tests_not_run: 原始报告为只读审查输出；未运行项保留在 finding.required_tests。

findings.title: Invalid base_ref silently produces an empty changed scope

evidence.result: git_changed_files only runs the explicit base diff when git_ref_exists(explicit_base) is true; missing refs and CalledProcessError are silently ignored, then the function returns the remaining changed set.
build_changed_scope trusts governance.git_changed_files(base_ref); if it returns [], root_governance_changed is false and selected_projects is empty.
The required PR/push changed-only gate passes a base ref into lean_governance.py ci --changed-only --base-ref.
exit_code=0; output reported changed_files=[], selected_project_count=0, total_project_count=10.

reproduction: From repository root at HEAD 3944f62daff6287eeb2a7f086dd1594b78b9f311, run: python -B scripts\lean_governance.py changed-scope --base-ref definitely-not-a-real-ref-3944f62d
Observed: command exits 0 and returns an empty changed scope instead of failing closed on the invalid base ref.
findings.impact: A missing, mistyped, or unfetched base ref can make the changed-scope governance selector validate zero projects and no root-governance scope. In CI/manual changed-only runs this can create a false green required check and skip governance coverage for the actual diff.
findings.recommended_fix: Fail closed whenever an explicit base ref is provided but cannot be resolved, or when the base...HEAD diff command fails. Return a non-zero CI result with the failing ref and git stderr in the JSON summary. Keep local dirty-tree fallback only for calls with no explicit base ref.
required_tests: Unit test git_changed_files with an explicit invalid base_ref and git_ref_exists=false; assert it raises or returns an error state that makes the CLI non-zero.
Integration test lean_governance.py changed-scope --base-ref <invalid> exits non-zero.
Integration test lean_governance.py ci --changed-only --base-ref <invalid> exits non-zero and reports the invalid base ref.

findings.title: Root governance changes exclude required projects from changed-scope checks

evidence.result: root_governance.changed_scope_excluded_projects excludes EEI and arxiv-daily-push even though both are registered projects with ci_mode required later in the same file.
When any root governance file changes, build_changed_scope selects all projects except those in changed_scope_excluded_projects.
The test suite locks in that root governance changes exclude EEI and arxiv-daily-push and set all_projects_required=false.

reproduction: Not executed as a live root-change diff in this read-only pass.
Static path: change a root governance file such as scripts/lean_governance.py; build_changed_scope will omit EEI and arxiv-daily-push by design per the cited code and tests.
findings.impact: Root governance code, standards, workflow, or validator changes can merge without exercising two required projects. If the root contract or validator regresses for those projects, the ordinary required changed-scope check can remain green until a scheduled/manual full run catches it later.
findings.recommended_fix: Do not exclude required projects from root-governance changed-scope coverage. If latency is the reason, split root-change coverage into stable required matrix jobs or separate required checks for the heavy projects, but keep required-check continuity for every ci_mode=required project affected by root governance code.
required_tests: Update root-change scope tests to assert every ci_mode=required project is covered or has its own required companion check.
Add a regression test where a root validator change selects EEI and arxiv-daily-push, or verifies separate required jobs for them.
Add a workflow-level assertion that root governance changes cannot produce all_projects_required=false without an explicit alternate required check.

findings.title: Required governance workflow uses mutable action version tags

evidence.result: The workflow uses actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-python@v5 instead of immutable commit SHAs.
The generated-views artifact upload uses actions/upload-artifact@v4 by tag.
The CI attestation artifact upload also uses actions/upload-artifact@v4 by tag.

reproduction: Static review only; no exploit attempted.
Inspect .github/workflows/project-governance.yml and observe every third-party action reference is tag-pinned rather than SHA-pinned.
findings.impact: The governance required check depends on mutable external tags. A compromised or retargeted action tag could alter checkout, runtime setup, or artifact behavior while the workflow still appears to run the required Project Governance job.
findings.recommended_fix: Pin all GitHub Actions to full-length commit SHAs, keep human-readable comments for source versions, and manage updates through Dependabot/Renovate or a reviewed update process. Add a static guard so new uses: entries are SHA-pinned.
required_tests: Add a governance test or CI lint that parses .github/workflows/project-governance.yml and rejects uses: values not pinned to a 40-character commit SHA unless explicitly allowlisted.
Verify upload-artifact and setup/checkouts remain pinned after dependency update automation changes them.

findings.title: render --write has no READ_ONLY or CI mode guard

evidence.result: The root contract says READ_ONLY, REVIEW, PLAN, CI, and Hook execution must not modify repository files or run write-mode generators.
The standard says READ_ONLY/REVIEW/PLAN/CI/Hook repository writes are prohibited and only a selected target render task may use write mode.
render_project_files writes target human-entry files whenever write=True and content differs.
The CLI exposes render --write and dispatches args.write directly to render_registered_project without checking RUN_MODE, CI, task id, acceptance id, or selected write authorization.
Existing tests cover write behavior in temp directories but do not assert that READ_ONLY/CI contexts reject --write.

reproduction: NOT_RUN in this review because running render --write against the repository would modify tracked files.
Expected failing scenario: RUN_MODE=READ_ONLY or CI=true with python -B scripts/lean_governance.py render --project <project> --write currently has no guard before path.write_text.
findings.impact: A user, hook, or automation can accidentally invoke a write-mode generator from a read-only or CI context and overwrite tracked project human-entry files. The final zero-diff check in ci does not protect direct render invocations, and the contract is only documented, not enforced at the CLI boundary.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a single write authorization gate before any render write: reject --write when RUN_MODE is READ_ONLY, REVIEW, PLAN, CI, or when CI=true, unless an explicit IMPLEMENT/render task contract is present. Require project/task/acceptance metadata for write-mode render and include tests for each prohibited mode.
required_tests: Unit test render --write with RUN_MODE=READ_ONLY returns non-zero and writes no files.
Unit test render --write with CI=true returns non-zero and writes no files.
Positive test for the allowed IMPLEMENT render path with explicit project/task/acceptance metadata, using a temp project directory.
